parsed_questions.error_comment: Task requires drawing curved-arrow mechanisms which is not text-oriented
Task requires multi-step synthesis outline not fully transcribed as text

parsed_questions.question_text:
Predict the principal products expected (if any) for the following reaction sequences. For each, select the structure you want to be evaluated.

A) Reaction of N-methylbenzamide with CH3CO2H at 25 °C for 10 hrs.
B) Conversion of cyclohexanecarboxylic acid by SOCl2 at 85 °C, then reaction with 1 equiv LiAl(OtBu)3 and H2O work-up.
C) Reaction of 2 equivalents of 2-hydroxyphenol and 1 equivalent of acetone with acid catalyst.
D) Reaction of isobutyric acid labeled with D at the carboxyl group with diazomethane (CH2N2) in ether.
E) Reaction of bicyclic compound (containing two ketone groups) with NaH and EtOH followed by H2O work-up.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) no reaction; B) cyclohexanecarbaldehyde; C) bis(2-hydroxyphenyl)propan-2-ol (chiral diol product); D) isobutyl diazomethane labeled ester; E) hydroxy-substituted ethyl cyclopentanecarboxylate

parsed_questions.question_text:
A) The reaction coordinate diagrams for II and III are meant to represent 'extreme cases.' In the boxes on the right, draw the general functional groups that Loudon is referring to in II and III.

B) Loudon discusses two major factors that could affect the rate of carbonyl substitution. Use these two criteria to compare the rate of reaction of S-ethyl ethanethioate with NaOEt to the reaction of ethyl acetate with NaOEt, whose reaction coordinate diagram is shown in I of Figure 21.6. In the boxes below, explain how each factor would affect the reaction coordinate for the S-ethyl case, including annotated structures to support your arguments. Then sum up your conclusion by completing the statement below the boxes:

Circle One: The reaction of S-ethyl ethanethioate with NaOEt will proceed at a FASTER / SLOWER / SAME rate relative to the reaction of ethyl acetate with NaOEt.

C) Based on your arguments in part (b), inscribe an annotated reaction coordinate diagram for the thiol ester reaction in the box labeled IV above.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer:
A) II = general functional group: R-C(=O)-NH2 (amide group) ; III = general functional group: R-C(=O)-Cl (acid chloride group)

B) Criterion 1: The resonance structures of S-ethyl ethanethioate show overlap of sulfur 3p orbital with carbon 2p, which is weaker than oxygen 2p overlap in ethyl acetate resonance. This results in S-ethyl ethanethioate being less stable than ethyl acetate, increasing the energy level of starting material.

Criterion 2: The SEt- group is a better leaving group than OEt- because sulfur is larger and better stabilizes negative charge in intermediate collapse. Also, pKa of HSEt is lower than HOEt, making SEt- a weaker base and thus a better leaving group.

Conclusion: The reaction of S-ethyl ethanethioate with NaOEt proceeds at a FASTER rate relative to ethyl acetate with NaOEt.

C) Reaction coordinate diagram IV should be annotated to reflect the thiol ester reaction coordinate with a lower activation energy peak compared to III and II, reflecting better leaving group ability and less stable starting material.

parsed_questions.question_text: Using structures and percentages, describe the sulfur-containing molecular species when ethane thiol (CH3CH2SH) is dissolved in a pH 10.8 buffer solution. The equilibrium is shown between ethane thiol (A) and its conjugate base ethane thiolate (B) with Ka indicated between them. The pH - pKa = log([B]/[A]) calculation setup is shown with pH = 10.8 and pKa = 10.6. Percentages of [A] = 38.8% and [B] = 61.2% are also displayed as a reference.

Which of the compounds shown (three structures: a methyl acetate (CH3COOCH2CH3) with hydrogens labeled a, b, c; propanal (H3CCH2CH=O); and methyl propanoate (CH3CH2COOCH3)) fits the following spectral data? Mass Spec: m/z 88.05, IR: 1735 cm-1, 1H NMR: 4.13 ppm (quartet, 2H), 2.21 (singlet, 3H), 0.86 (triplet, 3H). Select the correct structure according to this spectral data.
